summary: Docker-based reproduction confirmed that Glances REST API exposes all system monitoring data without authentication by default. An unauthenticated HTTP request to /api/4/all, /api/4/processes, and /api/4/system endpoints returns sensitive system information including CPU, memory, process list with PIDs, and system hostname.
expected_security_impact: Unauthenticated HTTP requests to REST API endpoints should return complete system monitoring data including CPU stats, memory stats, process list, and system information without requiring any credentials.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, started Glances REST API server without authentication enabled, and successfully retrieved sensitive system data via unauthenticated HTTP requests to /api/4/all, /api/4/processes, and /api/4/system endpoints.
verification_method: The verification script performs unauthenticated HTTP GET requests to multiple API endpoints and checks for presence of 'cpu', 'mem', process 'pid' fields, and 'hostname' in responses. Success is confirmed when all sensitive data fields are present in responses without authentication.
evidence_produced_by_run: HTTP response from /api/4/all endpoint contains CPU and memory statistics without authentication
HTTP response from /api/4/processes endpoint contains process list with PIDs without authentication
HTTP response from /api/4/system endpoint contains system hostname without authentication
All endpoints accessible without any Authorization header or credentials

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag v4.5.1, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
